parsed_review.summary: CP00-258 is a descriptor-only closeout pack for the RP07 search-core program covering all 40 planned units (RP07.P06.M03.S09-RP07.P06.M04.S23, risk class B). Reviewed via Read/Grep/Glob only. The pack does NOT assert runtime production_ready: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both set production_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review, production_ready_flag=search_core_permission_workflow_slice_descriptor_verified; this is consistent and weakens no production_ready gate. The descriptor-verified claim is fully supported: (1) deliverable/micro-title/phase distributions match across prompt, baseline, plan (closeout-pack-plan.json L412-423), CP258_REQUIREMENTS, and manifest (implementation 11, ui 8, claude_review 3, security_audit 8, test 8, hermes_evidence 2; M03=17 Primary, M04=23 Secondary); (2) service.js builds descriptor-only rows and freezeCp258Result pins ~40 runtime/write/bypass flags to the safe direction (no runtime permission eval, no audit write, no search/OCR/index/embedding/AI dispatch, no state/object-storage writes, no Hermes runtime receipt, synthetic_only, no_real_data, deny_over_allow_enforced); (3) validators.js enforces per-row descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer-safe/no-raw-payload plus ethical-wall, cross-tenant, leak, matched-rule, audit-event, Hermes-receipt and authority-boundary guards; (4) model.test.js asserts coverage.valid and slice.valid true, claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, read_only review; (5) the contract embeds the descriptor and validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs deep-equals it and pins current_pack=CP00-258; index.js re-exports via export *. CP178+ review hardening is encoded (forbidden_review_evidence rejects failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, plus claude_final_approval/source_mutation/enterprise_trust claims) and hardened_review_sequence keeps Claude review as a gated, non-final step. Authority boundary holds: Claude is read-only and not final approver; human approval required for enterprise trust; Hermes is used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt handling, not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false everywhere). Auth preflight passed with valid JSON; pre-review state is clean (no stale/forged claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection, raw-output, or receipt files). A repo-wide source sweep found no override setting runtime_execution/descriptor_only/permission_bypass/cross_*_allowed/leak_detected to an unsafe value. Unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 .docx, new-session-handoff.md, and the LDIP invalid-attempt file) are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 cosmetic note on scope-metadata labeling. Final human approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists two untracked paths in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-258/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (L95-98) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (L100-105). The label 'unrelated' is in tension with simultaneously classifying the same paths as pack implementation/doc artifacts. This has no functional impact: the authoritative pack scope is active_pack_implementation_refs (the 8 tracked source files in packages/search + contracts + scripts), active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and the cp00-258 directory holds the pack's own manifest/command-evidence artifacts. The overlap appears to stem from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved enumerating all dirty/untracked paths left in place rather than strictly non-pack files.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the scope generator so unrelated_dirty_files_preserved excludes paths already classified as untracked_implementation_files (or rename it to dirty_files_preserved) to avoid reviewer confusion. No change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational note. Does not affect descriptor-only correctness, validation, boundaries, or the closeout decision. No action required before closeout.
